message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implementing click.edit() multi-file support (str | Iterable[str] | None filename param). These are the only files I need to touch.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implementing process_priority param for click.edit()/Editor class. These are the only files my feature needs. What files does your feature touch?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Our features both touch edit()/Editor in the same two files, so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Proposing a sequential split instead: I'll implement multi-file filename support first (small, localized changes to edit_file->edit_files signature and the edit()/Editor call sites), send DONE when finished, then you layer process_priority on top of my changes rather than reverting them. I will avoid touching process_priority-related code; please avoid reverting the filename Iterable support when you edit. Sound OK?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Both features need the same 2 files (edit() signature + Editor class), so true file-level disjointness isn't possible. Proposing region-level split instead: I will ONLY append a new process_priority parameter at the END of the edit()/Editor.__init__ signatures (after your filename param changes) and add isolated, self-contained logic (a small helper for setting priority) that doesn't touch your filename-handling code paths. Please keep your filename param edits to their own signature position/logic so our additive changes don't collide. If that works for you, reply ACCEPT.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed. I'll implement filename Iterable[str] support now (edit_file->edit_files rename, subprocess call joining quoted filenames, type overloads in termui.py). I won't touch process_priority. Will send DONE when finished so you can layer your additive param on top.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed: you implement multi-file filename support first and send DONE, then I layer process_priority on top without touching your filename/Iterable logic. Waiting for your DONE now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished multi-file support: renamed Editor.edit_file -> edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]), joins multiple quoted filenames for the editor subprocess call, edit() now accepts filename: str | Iterable[str] | None with type overloads, docstring/versionchanged 8.2.0 added. I only added the new edit_files method and changed the filename normalization block at the end of termui.edit() (isinstance check + list conversion + ed.edit_files(filenames) call) — signature/init untouched otherwise, so there's room at the end of the parameter lists for your process_priority param. All existing tests pass. Go ahead and layer your changes on top.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implemented process_priority param: added to Editor.__init__ (appended after extension), edit() in termui.py (appended after filename), a new _set_process_priority() helper + priority maps in _termui_impl.py, and a call to it right after subprocess.Popen in edit_file(). All additive/appended per our agreement -- didn't touch filename/Iterable logic. Note: in my sandbox the repo files don't reflect your edits (no shared filesystem), so I applied against the original base signatures; my changes only append new params/lines and shouldn't conflict with your filename normalization work at merge time. All existing tests pass. Full patch written to patch.txt.
